one_time_description: Large equity in long-term investments gain of $5,812,004 recognized in 2020 vs $1,549,010 in 2019, primarily driven by Stk'emlupsemc te Secwepemc Nation equity pickup of $3,438,939
economic_notes: Band operates logging, gravel pit (Painted Rock Aggregates & Contracting LLP), retail store and fuel sales (Big Sky Station Ltd.), natural resources management (Skeetchestn Natural Resources LLP), trucking (Skeetchestn Holdings LLP), management/consulting (Knucwentwecw Development Corporation). COVID-19 pandemic noted as a risk factor in the auditor's report. Smith/Curtis Specific Claim Settlement trust fund of ~$5.4M exists. BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP interest acquired in 2020.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 (ended March 31, 2020). Large surplus driven by $5,812,004 equity in long-term investments pickup (primarily Stk'emlupsemc te Secwepemc Nation $3,438,939 band share and Stk'emlupsemc Limited Partnership). Six entities consolidated (Operating, Trust, Capital, Social Housing funds plus incorporated entities) and nine GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. COVID-19 pandemic noted in auditor report but no adjustments made. Smith/Curtis Specific Claim Settlement trust of ~$5.4M held by Royal Trust. Auditor is Daley LLP of Kamloops BC. No remuneration schedule included in extracted text. Housing expenses split between Housing & Capital Projects ($352,544) and Social Housing ($276,705). Total government transfers estimated as ISC + Province of BC + CMHC + FNHC + FNESC + FPHCC + ASETS + SNTC = $2,764,368 + $363,343 + $100,751 + $2,325,620 + $320,017 + $124,916 + $17,015 + $70,836 = $6,086,866 plus Q'wemtsi'n Health Society $220,601 if considered government transfer = $6,307,467; figures may vary depending on classification of Q'wemtsi'n and Canadian Red Cross.
